title: Review Alarm Messages And Logged-In User On VISU_MANCONTROL Screen
summary: Use the VISU_MANCONTROL screen on the Operator Station HMI to inspect the informational banner for alarm or informational messages, navigate through available messages using the banner arrows, and verify the ID of the user currently logged in.
when_to_use: Use when an operator or support user needs to view alarm or informational messages shown on the VISU_MANCONTROL screen and confirm which user ID is currently logged in on that screen.
not_for: Do not use this procedure to interpret the meaning of messages, because the source only documents how to view and navigate them.
Do not use this procedure to change settings, reset faults, or operate manual controls on the VISU_MANCONTROL screen.

roles_and_responsibilities.responsibility: Open the VISU_MANCONTROL screen, review the informational banner, navigate through displayed messages, read the logged-in user ID, and record what is shown.

safety_notes: This is a screen inspection procedure derived from documented HMI display elements.
The source packet does not provide message interpretation guidance; record displayed information exactly as shown.

access_required: Access to the Operator Station HMI
Ability to open the VISU_MANCONTROL screen
system_preconditions: The VISU_MANCONTROL screen must be available on the Operator Station HMI.
external_references: Figure 4-23, Operator Station HMI Manual Control Screen

visual_references.description: Primary visual reference for the VISU_MANCONTROL screen, including the informational banner, message navigation arrows, and logged-in user ID area.
Reference for how to access the VISU_MANCONTROL screen from the operator station display controls.

steps.title: Open the VISU_MANCONTROL screen
steps.purpose: Access the documented manual control screen where the informational banner and logged-in user ID are displayed.
steps.instruction: Open the VISU_MANCONTROL screen on the Operator Station HMI.
steps.healthy_condition: The manual control screen is visible and matches the documented layout for Figure 4-23.
steps.failure_condition: The VISU_MANCONTROL screen cannot be opened or does not display as expected.

actions.label: Open VISU_MANCONTROL
actions.exact_action: Open the VISU_MANCONTROL screen on the Operator Station HMI.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Overall VISU_MANCONTROL layout, including the informational banner and logged-in user ID area.
Display controls showing access to the Visu_ManControl screen.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop or escalate if the VISU_MANCONTROL screen cannot be accessed from the Operator Station HMI.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: The listed control mapping includes F1 to "Visu_ManControl."
Figure 4-23 is the Operator Station HMI Manual Control Screen.

steps.title: Locate the informational banner
steps.purpose: Identify the screen area where alarms or informational messages are displayed.
steps.instruction: Locate the informational banner on the VISU_MANCONTROL screen.
steps.expected_result: The banner area used for alarms or informational messages is identified.
steps.healthy_condition: An informational banner is visible on the screen.
steps.failure_condition: The informational banner is not visible where expected on the screen.

actions.label: Find banner
actions.exact_action: Locate the informational banner that displays alarms or informational messages.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Informational banner area on the VISU_MANCONTROL screen.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if the informational banner is not displayed when expected.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: An informational banner displays alarms or informational messages.

steps.title: Observe the current banner message
steps.purpose: Capture the currently displayed alarm or informational message.
steps.instruction: Observe the current message shown in the informational banner.
steps.expected_result: The currently displayed message is visible for review.
steps.healthy_condition: A message is visible in the banner, or the banner is present and readable.
steps.failure_condition: The banner content is unreadable or does not show a message when one is expected.

actions.label: Read current message
actions.exact_action: Observe the current message shown in the banner.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Current alarm or informational message displayed in the banner.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if the banner is present but the message cannot be read.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: An informational banner displays alarms or informational messages.

steps.title: Navigate through additional banner messages
steps.purpose: Review other available messages in the informational banner when more than one message is present.
steps.instruction: Use the arrows on the right side of the banner to navigate through additional messages, if present.
steps.expected_result: Additional messages can be reviewed using the banner navigation arrows.
steps.healthy_condition: The arrows allow movement through available messages in the banner.
steps.failure_condition: The arrows do not navigate through messages as documented.

actions.label: Navigate banner messages
actions.exact_action: Use the arrows on the right side of the banner to navigate through additional messages, if present.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Arrows on the right side of the informational banner used to navigate messages.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if messages cannot be navigated with the banner arrows as documented.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: The arrows on the right side of the banner can be used to navigate through messages in the banner.

steps.title: Read the logged-in user ID
steps.purpose: Verify which user is currently logged in on the VISU_MANCONTROL screen.
steps.instruction: Locate and read the ID of the user currently logged in.
steps.expected_result: The currently logged-in user ID is visible and can be read.
steps.healthy_condition: A user ID is displayed in the logged-in user area of the screen.
steps.failure_condition: The logged-in user ID is not displayed when expected.

actions.label: Read logged-in user
actions.exact_action: Locate and read the ID of the user currently logged in.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Area of the VISU_MANCONTROL screen that displays the ID of the currently logged-in user.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if the logged-in user ID is not displayed when expected.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: The screen also displays the ID of the user currently logged in.

steps.title: Record displayed messages and user ID
steps.purpose: Preserve the observed screen information for troubleshooting or handoff.
steps.instruction: Record the displayed messages and the logged-in user ID exactly as shown.
steps.expected_result: The observed banner messages and logged-in user ID are documented exactly as displayed.
steps.healthy_condition: Recorded information matches the screen display without interpretation or modification.
steps.failure_condition: The displayed information cannot be fully captured or verified.

actions.label: Document observations
actions.exact_action: Record the displayed messages and the logged-in user ID exactly as shown.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Banner message area and logged-in user ID area while documenting exactly what is displayed.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if the displayed information cannot be captured exactly as shown.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: An informational banner displays alarms or informational messages. The arrows on the right side of the banner can be used to navigate through messages in the banner. The screen also displays the ID of the user currently logged in.

success_criteria: The VISU_MANCONTROL screen is opened successfully.
The informational banner is located and reviewed.
Any available banner messages are viewed, including additional messages navigated with the banner arrows when present.
The currently logged-in user ID is identified.
Displayed messages and the logged-in user ID are recorded exactly as shown.
healthy_conditions: The VISU_MANCONTROL screen displays the informational banner.
Banner arrows navigate through messages when multiple messages are present.
The logged-in user ID is displayed on the screen.
failure_conditions: The VISU_MANCONTROL screen cannot be accessed.
The informational banner is not visible or readable.
Banner arrows do not navigate messages as documented.
The logged-in user ID is not displayed when expected.
escalation_guidance: Escalate if messages cannot be navigated with the banner arrows as documented.
Escalate if the logged-in user ID is not displayed when expected.
Escalate if the VISU_MANCONTROL screen cannot be accessed or does not display as expected.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: VISU_MANCONTROL screen showing the informational banner, message navigation arrows, and logged-in user ID area.
Operator station display controls used to access the Visu_ManControl screen.

source_refs.quote_or_summary: An informational banner displays alarms or informational messages. The arrows on the right side of the banner can be used to navigate through messages in the banner. The screen also displays the ID of the user currently logged in.

missing_details: The source does not define the meaning or required response for specific alarm or informational messages.
The source does not specify where or how recorded observations should be logged.
The source does not provide a time estimate for completing this check.
The source does not state whether production stop or lockout/tagout is required for this screen review.
